notes.title: Neem has multiple names
notes.content: Neem has various names given by different cultures, including Arishta, Nimba, Nimbaca, and Neem.

notes.title: Neem is a common tree in India
notes.content: Neem is widely found in India and other parts of Asia and Africa.

notes.title: Neem has medicinal properties
notes.content: Neem leaves, bark, flowers, fruits, and roots can be used to make medicines for various diseases.

notes.title: Neem is useful for farmers too
notes.content: Neem trees are beneficial for farmers as they provide natural pest control and other uses.

notes.title: Neem has different names in Hindi
notes.content: In Hindi, Neem has various names like Nim, Leemba, Nimori, Nimbamond, Nimbe, and Nimo.

notes.title: Scientists give special names to Neem
notes.content: Scientists have given special names to Neem, such as 'bitter grace of God', 'nature's gift', and 'cleanliness-parting tree'.

notes.title: Neem has various uses
notes.content: Neem trees are useful for various purposes, including medicine, pest control, and other domestic uses.

notes.title: Neem is also known as Arishta
notes.content: In Sanskrit, Neem is called 'Arishta', meaning the one who cures diseases.

notes.title: Neem's name given by Iranians
notes.content: The name 'Neem' is given to the tree by the Iranians.

notes.title: Neem has multiple uses in medicine
notes.content: Neem leaves, bark, flowers, fruits, and roots can be used to make medicines for various diseases, including measles and itching.

notes.title: Neem trees are beneficial for farmers
notes.content: Neem trees provide natural pest control and other benefits for farmers, making them useful for agricultural purposes.

facts.fact: Neem is a common tree in India and other parts of Asia and Africa.
Neem has various names given by different cultures, including Arishta, Nimba, Nimbaca, and Neem.
Neem leaves, bark, flowers, fruits, and roots can be used to make medicines for various diseases.
Neem trees are beneficial for farmers as they provide natural pest control and other uses.
In Hindi, Neem has various names like Nim, Leemba, Nimori, Nimbamond, Nimbe, and Nimo.
Scientists have given special names to Neem, such as 'bitter grace of God', 'nature's gift', and 'cleanliness-parting tree'.
Neem is also known as Arishta in Sanskrit, meaning the one who cures diseases.
The name 'Neem' is given to the tree by the Iranians.
Neem leaves give comfort and do not allow germs to breed when used as a remedy for itching and measles.
Neem trees are also known as the 'magic tree' and 'tree of the twentieth century'.
